title: About
text:
This dashboard is a grouping and visual summary of Canada Energy Regulator’s EF2023 raw data.
It focuses on 2023-2050 projections and attempts to estimate the scale of corrections required to achieve net-zero targets (e.g., about -40% for crude oil total production in a global net-zero scenario).
Note on years: Years 2022 (latest current data), 2030 (interim near-net-zero target), 2035 (negotiated interim near-net-zero target as per the Canada-Alberta MOU), and 2050 (target year as per EF2023/the Canadian Net-Zero Emissions Accountability Act) are highlighted.
Note on tabs: Tabs are sorted by decreasing total quantity (as per value cards). This means the top category is listed second, following Total.
Hierarchy:
 ─┬ Production
  │ ├ Crude Oil
  │ │ ├ Total
  │ │ ├ (Upgraded Bitumen)
  │ │ ├ C5+
  │ │ ├ Conventional Heavy
  │ │ ├ Conventional Light
  │ │ ├ Field Condensate
  │ │ ├ In Situ Bitumen
  │ │ └ Mined Bitumen
  │ ├ Hydrogen
  │ │ ├ Total (calculated)
  │ │ ├ Biomass
  │ │ ├ Electrolysis
  │ │ └ Natural gas with CCS
  │ └ Natural Gas
  │   ├ Total
  │   ├ Solution
  │   ├ Non Associated
  │   ├ Tight
  │   ├ Shale
  │   ├ Coalbed Methane
  │   └ ...
  └ Prices
    ├ Benchmark Prices
    └ ... End-use Prices
...
Project timeline:

2026-02-26: Project started
2026-02-26: First tab (Production > Oil > Total) created
2026-02-27: Tab layout established
2026-02-28: Oil tab completed
2026-02-28: Production tab completed
2026-02-28: Added Financials section
2026-03-01: Financials > Benchmarks built
2026-03-01: Production > Crude Oil: Standardized CSS (incl. narrow screens)
2026-03-01: Standardized nomenclature. Added trend indicators for scenario cards
2026-03-02: All > graphs: Highlighted key years

…

title: Site example (Quarto)
text:
Footnotes


Low-sulfur, easier to refine↩︎
API gravity for the Brent benchmark is cited to be just above 38 degrees.↩︎
A density equivalent of around 824 kg/m³ (at 15°C)↩︎
High-sulfur, harder to refine↩︎
For more definitions, refer to CER’s Energy Information Program - Glossary↩︎
e.g., Below 15 US$/bbl↩︎
The reference for the 19% is calculated each year. The metric is also averaged across scenarios. Example 1: If one scenario halves the baseline and another keeps it unchanged, the metric is 0.25. This is because half of the baseline was subtracted by one of the two scenarios, adding 0.25 to the pooled metric.↩︎
For more definitions, refer to CER’s Energy Information Program - Glossary↩︎
